summary: Ambari Agent fails to start after cluster upgrade
description: After upgrading Ambari from 2.7.5 to 3.1.0, Ambari Agent fails to start on all nodes with 'Connection refused' error when trying to connect to Ambari Server. The agent logs show 'java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused' on port 8080. This prevents any service status reporting or configuration synchronization, rendering the cluster unmanageable. No workaround exists until the agent can reconnect to the server.

summary: HDFS NameNode fails to start after Ambari config sync
description: Following a configuration update via Ambari UI, HDFS NameNode fails to start with 'java.lang.NullPointerException' in namenode log. The error occurs during the loading of the fsimage. Ambari reports the service as 'Starting' indefinitely, and the cluster becomes inaccessible for any HDFS operations. No manual recovery is possible without restarting the Ambari Server and rolling back configurations.

summary: Ambari Server crashes on blueprint deployment
description: When deploying a blueprint with HBase and Kafka services, Ambari Server crashes with 'OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space'. The server process terminates abruptly, and no recovery is possible without manual restart. This blocks all cluster provisioning and reconfiguration workflows. The issue occurs consistently with blueprints containing more than 10 services.

summary: YARN ResourceManager fails to initialize after configuration change
description: After updating YARN's 'yarn.resourcemanager.hostname' via Ambari, ResourceManager fails to start with 'java.io.IOException: Cannot open channel to x at /host:23145'. The error is logged repeatedly in rm log, and the service remains in 'Starting' state. This blocks all YARN job submissions and cluster scheduling, and no recovery is possible without manual config rollback.

summary: Ambari fails to stop services during cluster decommission
description: During decommission of a host via Ambari, the system hangs indefinitely with services in 'Stopping' state. The Ambari Server log shows a deadlock in 'HostComponentImpl' thread, and the UI becomes unresponsive. This prevents safe removal of the host and blocks all subsequent cluster operations. No workaround exists without manually killing Ambari processes.

summary: ZooKeeper service fails to start after Ambari restart
description: After a planned Ambari Server restart, ZooKeeper fails to start with 'java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Invalid port in client configuration' in zk log. Ambari reports the service as 'Starting' indefinitely. This breaks dependency chains for HBase, Kafka, and other services, rendering the cluster inoperable. No manual fix is available without re-deploying ZooKeeper via Ambari.

summary: Ambari UI becomes unresponsive after configuration save
description: After saving HDFS configuration changes via Ambari UI, the web interface becomes completely unresponsive with '500 Internal Server Error' in browser console. The Ambari Server log shows 'java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded'. This blocks all cluster management tasks, and no UI access is possible until the server is restarted. No workaround is available.

summary: Knox Gateway fails to start after Ambari upgrade
description: After upgrading Ambari from 2.7.4 to 3.0.0, Knox Gateway fails to start with 'java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: org.apache.hadoop.gateway.filter.GatewayFilter'. The Ambari UI shows the service as 'Starting' indefinitely. This prevents access to secured Hadoop services and blocks all external API access. No recovery is possible without manual redeployment.

summary: HBase Master fails to start after Ambari configuration sync
description: After applying a configuration update via Ambari, HBase Master fails to start with 'java.lang.NullPointerException' in hbase log. The Ambari UI shows the service as 'Starting' and remains in that state. This blocks all HBase operations and cluster access. No workaround is available without rolling back the configuration change and restarting the Ambari Server.
